assertions.text: Identifies DI scope mismatch: IHostedService is singleton, DbContext is scoped — must use IServiceProvider.CreateScope()
assertions.evidence: with_skill explicitly states 'Inject IServiceProvider (not scoped services directly)' and 'Create scope per work cycle'. without_skill mentions IOptions but misses the singleton/scoped lifetime mismatch entirely.

assertions.text: Recommends PeriodicTimer over Task.Delay for background services
assertions.evidence: with_skill explicitly recommends PeriodicTimer. without_skill only says 'Configure periodic execution using Timer or PeriodicTimer' without explaining why.

assertions.text: Identifies EF Core NoTracking + explicit Update() pattern for background service writes
assertions.evidence: with_skill covers NoTracking by default, AsTracking for writes, and ExecuteUpdateAsync. without_skill only mentions async/await generally.

assertions.text: Recommends IOptions<T> for SMTP config instead of raw IConfiguration
assertions.evidence: Both mention IOptions pattern. without_skill also covers this.

assertions.text: Mentions CancellationToken must be passed through entire chain including EF Core calls
assertions.evidence: Both mention CancellationToken. with_skill is more specific about passing stoppingToken through entire chain.

evals.verdict: WITH SKILL clearly better — catches .NET-specific DI lifetime pitfall and EF Core patterns that baseline misses
evals.issue: with_skill output is too verbose (50+ items, 7 phases). Plan mode should focus on top critical .NET-specific items, not generate exhaustive list.

assertions.text: Identifies all methods are sync (should be async) with CancellationToken
assertions.evidence: Both identify missing async. with_skill provides specific fix with CancellationToken parameter.

assertions.text: Identifies SaveChanges inside loop as critical issue
assertions.evidence: Both identify this. with_skill provides ExecuteUpdateAsync (EF Core 7+) as better alternative.

assertions.text: Identifies missing AsNoTracking on read-only queries
assertions.evidence: with_skill explicitly flags GetOrder and GetPendingOrders missing AsNoTracking. without_skill does not mention this.

assertions.text: Identifies IConfiguration injection should be IOptions<T>
assertions.evidence: with_skill flags IConfiguration as moderate issue and provides IOptions<OrderServiceOptions> fix. without_skill completely misses this.

assertions.text: Recommends NoTracking by default in DbContext configuration
assertions.evidence: with_skill recommends configuring QueryTrackingBehavior.NoTracking in DbContext. without_skill does not mention this.

evals.verdict: WITH SKILL clearly better — catches IOptions<T>, AsNoTracking, and NoTracking-by-default patterns that baseline misses entirely

issues_to_fix: Plan mode output too verbose (50+ items). Add guidance to focus on top critical .NET-specific items from Core Guardrails first, not generate exhaustive list.
Plan mode should distinguish between 'critical items commonly missed by AI' vs 'general best practices' — skill should emphasize the former.
